entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The crop being screened for resistance to soil-borne diseases.

entities.name: COLLAR ROT
entities.description: A disease caused by Sclerotium rolfsii affecting the collar region of the plant.

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing collar rot.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE SCREENING METHOD
entities.description: A rapid and reliable method developed to screen crops for disease resistance.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a cultivar to withstand or resist the effects of a pathogen.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT SHELLS
entities.description: A substrate used for the growth and formation of sclerotia of the pathogen.

entities.name: SORGHUM STRAW
entities.description: A substrate compared against groundnut shells for pathogen growth.

entities.name: SAND-MAIZE MEAL
entities.description: A substrate compared against groundnut shells for pathogen growth.

entities.name: POTATO DEXTROSE BROTH
entities.description: A substrate compared against groundnut shells for pathogen growth.

entities.name: SEED ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting the seeds of the crop.

entities.name: DAMPING OFF
entities.description: A disease causing seedlings to collapse and die.

entities.name: RIZOLEX
entities.description: A chemical seed treatment used to control seed rot and collar rot.

entities.name: THIRAM
entities.description: A chemical seed treatment used in combination with Rizolex to control seed rot and collar rot.
